text: Authors must be responsible for what has been written in return for the credit and recognition that authorship brings.

For any multi-authored article based on a student thesis, research study or dissertation, the student should normally be the first author, unless there is documented evidence that the journal required that the authorship should be written in a different order.
However, the Chair of the student’s Program Committee should serve as the corresponding author with any journal.

text: CREDIT POLICY (12) If fees remain unpaid after the deadline for adding/dropping of courses of the semester/term in which a student was ‘suspended due to financial inability’, the student shall be recommended for dismissal.

Once dismissed, students who are staying in campus accommodation shall be asked to vacate their dormitory rooms; and their educational visas will be cancelled. (14) ‘Dismissed’ students can no longer avail of student privileges and benefits after their dismissal.

Permanent dismissal will be applied to those who will be dismissed again due to non-payment of fees on time. (17) Graduating students without financial clearance will not be allowed to take the final defense of their dissertation/thesis/research study/project/ internship. (18) Graduating students with unpaid fees will not be permitted to graduate nor will they be allowed to rent gowns, and join the graduation rehearsal and ceremony.
Sponsors must be invoiced for outstanding dues and requested to send AIT a promissory note detailing when and how payment will be made.
Writing-off of doubtful accounts will be requested by OFIN and have to be approved by the President, or their VP designate.

(3) Where both parents are entitled to receive assistance (discount/subsidy) with fees for the education of dependent children, the lower discount / subsidy rate applies. text: With regard to parents (AIT students and employees) with more than one child: (1) Fees for the first child shall be paid in full within 30 days from the first day of the semester. (1) Fees for the rest of the children shall be paid in full by the end of the second month of the semester.
With regard to other students, fees shall be paid in full within 30 days from the first day of the semester.
If the payment is not received within the periods stipulated, the AITIS students concerned shall be removed from the rolls of the school and shall not be allowed to attend classes.
Lessees are required to pay a three-month advance deposit to take on the lease of premises.
Dining (1) Personal meals are required to be settled.
Invoices are required to be settled by the indicated due date.
Overdue accounts shall be reviewed periodically and follow-up on collections shall be initiated.
Any concerns involving student welfare that is related to accommodation should be brought to the attention of the Director of Student Affairs.
For subsequent semesters, these students may opt to reside off-campus.
Married Students are permitted to bring their immediate families (spouse and children) to join them during their studies.
Families of non-resident students may not reside on campus if the non-resident student is off-campus.
Master’s or doctoral students with spouses who are AIT employees must register for staff accommodation.
Students should move with the spouse when staff accommodation becomes available.

Exceptions to this may only be given to those whose exchange program is less than two months.
All guests must be registered with the Accommodation Unit three days before arrival.
With prior approval of the Accommodation Unit, guests may stay in student dormitories for a maximum period of one week per semester.

Regular cleaning and hygiene of the unit has to be maintained by the student.
OFAM is authorized to undertake random inspection and may lay sanctions if the unit is found to be dirty and unhygienic.

text: Cooking is not permitted, except in accommodation units where cooking facilities or shared cooking facilities are provided.
Students are allowed to install outdoor antennas or cable TV, only with the prior agreement of the Accommodation Unit.
Students are not allowed to install heavy appliances, such as air-conditioners, in accommodation units that are not designed for such facilities, i.e. in Student Villages and Category 1 dorms.

Upon leaving the accommodation unit, students are required to leave their units clean, remove all possessions, and to leave the unit and its furniture and equipment in the same condition as when the tenancy began.
Students shall not disturb fellow students in the residential areas.
Games and activities shall cease at 2300 hours at all sports fields and courts in and near campus residential areas.

text: Noisy group studies and parties are not allowed in student accommodation.
Alternative rooms designated for such purpose must be used.
Students who misplace their accommodation unit keys may ask the Accommodation Unit to open their dorms.
Furniture and appliances should not be removed from the accommodation unit.
Repair and replacement of furniture and appliances must be requested from and Page 6/8

CAMPUS ACCOMMODATION FOR STUDENTS approved by the Accommodation Unit, which may ask the tenant to pay additional charges, in situations where damage to furniture and appliances has resulted from direct negligence by the tenant.
Students who cook in dormitories where cooking is prohibited may face a minimum fine of Baht 2,000, or the actual costs of the damage caused by cooking.

Students who install heavy appliances, such as air-conditioners, in accommodation units not designed for such facilities, will be fined Baht 2,000 and charged for the services related to the removal of the appliances.
Students found guilty may face a fine of Baht 2,000, or the actual costs of the damage caused by smoking or burning.
Pets are not allowed in student accommodation.
Students disturbing the peace of their surroundings shall receive a formal reprimand as a first warning.
Repeated disturbances may face further sanctions.
Rooms must be used for residential purposes only.

Students who do not maintain the upkeep and cleanliness of their bedroom, bathroom, corridor, balcony, and cooking facilities, where provided, will be fined Baht 2,000 and charged for cleaning services of the unit.
Self-funded students are required to pay a one-month advance deposit.
Special and exchange students are required to pay the rent in full for the whole duration of their stay on campus.

text: A Grievance Committee shall receive all grievances.
The Grievance Committee shall elect a Chair from among its members, which will have a term of two years and can serve for not more than two consecutive terms.
The Grievance Committee shall perform three main roles: a.
The Grievance Committee must be prepared to identify breaches of the Institute P&Ps, etc.
deontic_formula: O(prepared(GrievanceCommittee) and identify(GrievanceCommittee, breaches) and of(breaches, InstitutePandPs))

text: In performing their duties, they must protect the interests of all members of the AIT community. c.
The Grievance Committee should express in writing what came to light during the processing of grievances.
They must inform the President of all settlements made during the grievance process.

A complainant is required to initiate the grievance process by submitting the grievance in writing (please refer to the Grievance / Complaint Fact Sheet in the Annex as a guide) to the Grievance Committee or discussing their grievance in person with any member of the Grievance Committee. b.

When there are strong grounds to conclude that a serious crime such as rape, grievous bodily harm, assault, or theft has been committed, the Grievance Committee should consult with the President, and if necessary, inform the police.
The complainant may seek the advice of the Grievance Committee on what to say or write. f.
The person(s) towards whom the direct confrontation is directed may likewise use consultation and direct confrontation in their response. g.

predicates.subject: person
predicates.condition: towards whom the direct confrontation is directed

text: Outcomes of the mediation shall include explicit written agreements to be submitted to the Grievance Committee. m.

If the informal grievance process, fails to lead to a satisfactory resolution of the complaint, the complainant who has suffered from harassment, abuse or discrimination may lodge a formal complaint with the Grievance Committee.
The formal complaint must be in writing, and should state clearly all facts for consideration. c.

The grievance must include: i. the alleged violation and/or misapplication of a stated policy or procedure; ii. a statement of the relevant facts supporting the complainant’s position, e.g., date(s), event(s), name(s)/title of person(s) involved, witnesses; and iii. the remedy sought by the complainant d.
The Grievance Committee is required to address the complaint in utmost confidentiality and with due regard to the privacy of the complainant, and of the person accused (‘the defendant’). e.
fol_expansion: ∀x (GrievanceCommittee(x) → O(confidentiality(x, complaint) ∧ dueRegardToPrivacy(x, complainant) ∧ dueRegardToPrivacy(x, defendant)))

text: When the Grievance Committee is involved in a grievance procedure, they should keep in mind that facts are the substance of any grievance, and the fact-gathering process is of fundamental importance.
fol_expansion: (exists y)(grievanceCommitteeMember(y) and grievanceProcedureInvolvement(y) implies (exists z)(fact(z) and gatheringProcess(z, y)))

text: In gathering the facts, the Grievance Committee should put all points in writing even if the grievance is not presented.
In obtaining evidence, the Grievance Committee should concentrate on recording facts, not arguments.
Having gathered the relevant facts, the Grievance Committee should proceed to analyze the grievance.
When further action is required, the President shall appoint a Grievance Tribunal. b.
The written notification of the hearing should give: the time and place of the hearing; ● the name(s) of the complainant(s); ● Page 8/11

text: The Grievance Tribunal shall be convened at the earliest possible date after the formal complaint.
Hearings shall be open only to the members of the Grievance Tribunal, the complainant(s) and the defendant, and any witnesses called. b.
fol_expansion: ∀x (Person(x) ∧ (x is a member of Grievance Tribunal or x is the complainant or x is the defendant or x is a witness called)(x) → O(attendHearing(x)))

predicates.subject: person

text: The Grievance Tribunal must seek to ascertain, to the best of its ability, the facts of the case. c.
In ascertaining the facts of the case, the Tribunal may call witnesses to testify before the Grievance Tribunal.
After having received the report of the Grievance Tribunal, the President may take further legal advice, before acting. g.
The President may delegate their responsibilities to a Vice President.

When the defendant is an employee, pending the report of the Grievance Tribunal, the President may, if they consider such action justified, suspend the defendant from work until completion of the investigation. b.
A copy of the report shall be kept in the Office of the President, and be accessible to the immediate Supervisor, Director of OHRS, Director of OSA, or the Director of OAA.

text: The Grievance Tribunal will recommend whether a copy of the report should be placed in the defendant’s file in OHRS or OSA.
If either the complainant or the defendant is not satisfied with the decision of the President, they may appeal to the Board, through its Audit Committee.
If make-up classes are scheduled out of regular class hours, the re-scheduled classes should not clash with the schedules of students in the class.
Students have the right to expect that their grade in a course should be determined only by academic achievement consistent with the aims and content of that course.

text: STUDENT CODE OF CONDUCT Students at AIT are expected to meet the highest standards of personal, ethical and moral conduct.

If there is suspicion of cheating during an examination, in an assignment or a research project, the instructor/invigilator should immediately call the attention of the student and put a notation on segments of the exam, assignment or project where cheating has been suspiciously committed.

The instructor/invigilator should seek an explanation from the student at the end of the examination or when the assignment or project was submitted, and submit an incident report to the Department Head, who should then notify the School Dean.
Notes of the interview will be recorded and should be agreed upon by all parties.
If the interview panel is satisfied that there is no evidence of cheating, the Department Head and the instructor/invigilator may recommend to the School Dean to give no penalty.
fol_expansion: ∀x ∀y ∀z (DepartmentHead(x) ∧ InstructorOrInvigilator(y) ∧ SchoolDean(z) ∧ noEvidenceOfCheating(y) → P(recommendPenalty(x, y, z)))

text: However, if cheating was proven, the Department Head and the instructor/invigilator may recommend to the School Dean to disregard the original examination score and require the student to submit an equivalent piece of work or retake a different version of the examination.
fol_expansion: ∀x ∀y ∀z ((DepartmentHead(x) ∧ InstructorOrInvigilator(y) ∧ Student(z) ∧ ProvenCheating(z)) → O(disregardOriginalScore(x, y, z)))

text: Upon receipt of the retake, the School Dean may decide to: ● Downgrade by 1 step the grade the student will receive upon repetition of the exam or resubmission of the assignment/project; ● Give a score of ‘zero’ for the specific exam, project or assignment; or ● Give a failing grade or “F” for the course; or ● Recommend the student for suspension or dismissal.
The appeal should be addressed to the School Dean.

If School Dean feels that there is evidence of serious dishonesty and the recommendation of the Field of Study Coordination and instructor/invigilator was suspension or dismissal, the matter should be elevated to the Vice President for Academic Affairs.

predicates.condition: evidenceOfSeriousDishonesty AND recommendationOfFieldOfStudyCoordinationAndInstructorInvigilator is suspension or dismissal

text: Students who are allowed to extend beyond the normal study period have the following privileges:
Before a student is accepted for enrolment, the Admissions and Scholarships Unit (ASU) is required to ensure that there is proof of financial support for the student’s full study program.
For self-paying students, the proof can be in the form of bank statement, cash, etc. and for external/sponsored students, authorized documentary proof of sponsorship must be submitted.

With regard to self-paying students and students with external sponsors, first semester fees must be paid in advance and/or fully paid up upon enrolment, otherwise they will not be allowed to register.
If fees remain unpaid by the start of a semester, a student’s status shall be ‘suspended due to financial inability’.

Thus, they cannot enroll in any course nor can they avail of student privileges and benefits during the suspended period such as use of AIT ID card, access to SIS and registration record, staying in AIT accommodation, use of Library and School facilities, discount of children in AITIS, etc.

No member of the AIT community, trustee, faculty, staff, or student, including outreach program participants, should act deliberately or thoughtlessly to interfere with the study, work or home environment of other members of the community.
Those with academic or administrative supervisory authority, permanent or temporary, over faculty, staff or students, should use such authority with respect,

The opportunities, services, facilities and support offered by AIT should be compatible with the purposes of the Institute, and be open impartially to everyone in the community according to qualification, performance and achievement.

There should be no denial or granting of rights or privileges wholly or partly, on irrelevant grounds such as race, nationality, gender, creed, age, marital status, handicap, or physical characteristics.
Sexual harassment 1.1 Given the unique diversity of the Institute community, AIT must develop special awareness and sensitivity to deter or respond to the problem of sexual harassment.
supervisor:employee) may not constitute sexual harassment, individuals must declare such a conflict of interest and remove themselves from the situation.
It must be recognized by all concerned that breaches of confidentiality or privacy in the informal or formal response to a complaint may divert or even redirect the remedial action concerned.
1.5 Reprisals against an individual, who in good faith reports or provides information in an investigation of conduct that may violate Institute policy, will not be tolerated.
False accusations or false evidence offered in the remedial process may themselves be considered grounds for disciplinary action.

1.7 Individuals who are found to have violated the Institute's policies on harassment, abuse or discrimination, will be subject to disciplinary action decided by the President, taking account of the disciplinary guidelines of other appropriate Institute bodies with Thai Labor, and where appropriate, Thai Civil or Criminal Law.

1.8 Nothing in this policy should be construed as abridging the authority of the President of the Institute or of presidential designees to an immediate emergency remedies to ensure the maintenance of order in the event of Institute disruption, disturbance of the ongoing business and educational mission of the Institute, or threats to life or property.

predicates.subject: president
predicates.condition: in an immediate emergency, the Institute is disrupted, there is a disturbance of the ongoing business and educational mission of the Institute, or threats to life or property

text: 2.3 Preliminary consultation should be available to any member of the Institute community who wishes to discuss issues related to problem areas.

2.4 Direct communication refers to the action of an individual who chooses to act on perceived harassment, abuse or discrimination by addressing the other party or parties directly, or writing a statement to the individual(s) concerned, describing the unwelcome behavior and its effect and stating that the behavior must cease.
2.5 Direct communication may sometimes follow consultation, the complainant seeking the advice of one of the advisors listed in Section C-2.3 above, on what to say or write.
The person(s) to whom the direct communication is made may likewise use consultation and direct communication in their response.
Formal procedures 3.1 A complainant may, with or without previous recourse to informal channels, decide to seek redress through formal grievance procedures.

predicates.subject: complainant
predicates.condition: with or without previous recourse to informal channels

text: 3.3 Formal complaint statements should be made as soon as possible; the earlier the report, the easier it is to investigate and take appropriate remedial action.
3.4 The person(s) against whom the complaint has been initiated must be informed immediately of the complaint by the appointed grievance committee, and be provided with opportunity for rebuttal.

Such an investigation may only be initiated on the instructions of the President or his designee, and after the complainee(s) has (have) received a written general description of the perceived conduct and the provisions which they are claimed to violate.

3.6 Matters preliminary to any hearing should normally be decided and notice of the hearing given as soon as possible, certainly no later than five working days after the grievance committee has been informed of the case concerned, and not less than 72 hours prior to the hearing.
Hearings should normally be held within ten working days of the notice.

predicates.condition: within ten working days of the notice

text: Findings and recommendations are confidential and shall not be made public by the Institute or by any participant in a hearing, including the

predicates.subject: instance
predicates.condition: in a hearing or by any participant

text: The separation of one or more cases from a group of cases previously set for a consolidation hearing should not, however, be considered to affect the remaining cases in the group.

If no satisfactory explanation for the absence is received, or if a complainee leaves a hearing before its conclusion or adjournment, the hearing proceed without the individual(s) concerned, and the committee may reach a conclusion based on the available evidence and make its report accordingly.

3.11 Any relevant evidence may be admitted if it is of the kind on which responsible persons are accustomed to rely in the conduct of serious affairs, but all evidence admitted must be justified before it can be used to explain or supplement other evidence.
No evidence, other than that discussed at the hearing, should be considered by a grievance committee.

3.13 Following the presentation and examination of evidence at the hearing, a grievance committee shall agree in private its written report to the President, which must be submitted within two working days of the termination of the hearing.
The Report of the grievance committee shall summarize the facts, and make a determination as to whether the individual(s) concerned did or did not commit the acts alleged.
The President's decision shall be final.

The Institute recognizes the importance of health and safety of its students, thus, to further enhance and improve students’ life and study, the Institute observes the following policies related to substance abuse. ● AIT is obligated to comply with the laws of the host country.
This policy shall be implemented to enforce and monitor the laws of the host country. ● All students, faculty and staff are expected to take responsibility for their own actions.
The AIT Medical Clinic shall serve as the unit for referral for treatment of substance abuse problems.
The Institute shall be guided by the following:
All incidents of alcohol violations shall be reported to the Director, Office of Student Affairs in case of students. c.

Disciplinary Action: (1) Warning/written reprimand to be placed on student file (2) Community service (3) Suspension for 1 semester if behavior leads to injury (4) Dismissal/Expulsion The student may appeal thru the Director, Office of Student Affairs to the President.
deontic_formula: O(placeWarningReprimand(student) v O(performCommunityService(student)) v O(imposeSuspension(student, 1, 'semester')) v O(enforceDismissalExpulsion(student)))

Responsibility centers are required to produce internal systems and documentation for implementing this policy locally.
Individuals are required to abide by rules and requirements made under the authority of this policy.

text: All guests must be registered with the Accommodation Unit three days before arrival.
With prior approval of the Accommodation Unit, they may stay in student dormitory for a maximum quota of one week per semester.
In case of over staying guests, special prior permission must be obtained from the Director, Office of Facilities and Asset Management (OFAM) and a daily rent will be charged for the room.
The Institute shall in no way be held responsible for the legal status of the guests.
Visas, residence permits, etc. shall be the responsibility of the guests and not the Institute.
Students may put their names in the waiting list and live in staff housing depending on availability.
They are allowed to temporarily stay in such units until the period provided by the Accommodation Unit or until such time that they have been allocated a room in student accommodation.
Students are required to vacate their rooms within five days (including weekends) after their graduation.
New and continuing students wishing to move in another category of rooms may queue during the period designated by the Accommodation Unit.
· Only continuing students, who are off-campus on data collection or on approved leave, may put their names on the waiting list through email to accommodation@ait.ac.th.

Transfer/Relocation According to Waiting List · An offer for transfer/relocation from one unit to another according to the waiting list shall start during the period designated by the Accommodation Unit and is done every Saturday. · Allocation by waiting list is done only during the semester.

text: Failure to accept the allocated dorm shall result in the removal of the student from the waiting list. · A student wishing to move to a new room MUST settle all outstanding dues related to the previous room, before being assigned a new room.
New and continuing students may apply for student villages following the policies below:

Shared Unit for Unmarried Students · Students may apply for a shared unit provided they are able to find a group of three students in their batch (i.e. with similar entry and graduation dates) who mutually agree to live together.

A change in the grouping after allocation is not permitted unless otherwise approved by the Director, OFAM. · If shared units become available, the groups at the head of the waiting list will be notified.

text: Cooking is not permitted, except in accommodation units rooms where cooking arrangement is facilities or shared cooking facilities are provided.
In units where cooking is not permitted, shared cooking facilities will be explored, when Institute space and resources become available.
Students are allowed to install outdoor antennas or cable TV, only with the prior agreement of the Accommodation Unit.
Students are not allowed to install heavy appliances, such as air- conditioners, in accommodation units that are not designed for such facilities, i.e. in Student Villages and Category 1 dorms.
Students shall not disturb fellow students in the residential area.
Games and activities shall cease at 2300 hours in all sports fields and courts in and near campus residential areas.
Noisy group studies and parties are not allowed in student accommodation.
Alternative rooms designated for such purpose must be used.
Students who misplace their accommodation unit keys may ask the Accommodation Unit to open their dorms.
Furniture and appliances should not be removed from the accommodation unit.

accommodation units dormitories not designed for such facilities, will be fined Baht 2,000 and charged for the services related to the removal of the appliances.

Students disturbing the peace of their surroundings shall receive a formal reprimand as a first warning.

Rooms must be used for residential purposes only.

text: While some of you are very fluent speakers of English, at AIT all degree program students are expected to become competent academic writers.
Therefore, all AIT degree program students are required to fulfill the English requirements before they can graduate.
If you score above a 6.0 on the AWPT, you will automatically be exempted from having to take mandatory EL courses.
If you score 5.0, you are required to attend and successfully complete EL 15 and EL 19 before you can graduate.
If you score 5.5, you are required to attend and successfully complete EL 19 before you can graduate.
All students must have their advisor approve and sign the title page of their research report, thesis or dissertation, and then submit the signed title page to the LC for title-checking.
Finally, they must submit the title page signed by their advisor and by LC to the AIT Registry.

When using Internet, students have to adhere to the Institute’s Policies & Procedures on the Use of Computing and Network Services at AIT whose details can be viewed from http://intranet.ait.ac.th/administration/policies-and-procedures/AA/aa-9-4-1- use-of-computing-and-network-services-at-agt-7jun2021.pdf Internet access from academic/administrative buildings is restricted for academic purposes only.
Nevertheless, adherence to the host country’s (Thai) laws still must be strictly observed.
UIA subscription is mandatory for every student residing on campus and its current fee of 400 Baht/month is monthly collected with accommodation rental fee.

Violation to Institute’s Policies & Procedures on the Use of Computing and Network Services at AIT or unlawful use of UIA, will be penalized ranging from blocking Internet access for a period of various length depending on its severity and frequency up to expulsion from the Institute.

Students may also avail of Microsoft Office 365 licenses through Microsoft’s Student Advantage Program where every student is entitled to install Microsoft Office 365 on up to 5 devices, including PC, Mac, Tablets and Smart Phones.

text: Details of IT services mentioned above as well other information are available on Helpdesk website https://helpdesk.ait.ac.th/ Students encountering difficulties in using Internet and other campus wide IT services above shall contact Helpdesk preferably by sending email to helpdesk@ait.ac.th.
Those insured under the Generali Medical Insurance should present their Generali Card before consulting with the doctor for the diagnosis of any illness.
In emergency cases or if any medical emergency assistance is required, the nurse on duty can be contacted at 02-524-
You should not work alone in the laboratory, especially at night, without written permission from the Laboratory Supervisor.
If you have to use an outside technician, ask for a company ID card.

Please extend your cooperation to the security guards on duty when an ID card is demanded. ● Your guests should know your personal details (name, house/room number, telephone, School/Program, etc.), for security reasons. ● Keep noise down and do not disturb peace on campus especially during the late hours. ● Lock your bicycles even if they are old.

text: The offender is required to present an AIT or valid ID card to the security on duty.
If ID card is not available, other form of identification should be provided.
You may prefer to bring a bicycle with you.

COMMUTING AND TRAVELS WITHIN/OUTSIDE THAILAND AIT Transport In special circumstances, students are permitted to use AIT vehicles in connection with their research or for some official student activities.
In all such cases, the reservation of the vehicle must be made by a member of the faculty or a senior administrative staff member in the School or by the Student Union President to the Motor Pool.
